column17: I am a staff member at THI
column32: clear spatial layout, good mobility, good lighting, digital infrastructure, ventilation options
column40: Constant construction zones around campus make mobility across buildings very limited. I am not mobility impaired but THI design decisions like heavy-weight doors can be a nightmare for folks in weelchairs or some other physical handicaps. The design choices for blinds in buildings that don't have fully working A/C e.g. in K building or B building turn classes and office areas into saunas where normal work is impossible for prolonged periods of time and if certain handicaps conditions e.g. respiratory issues, it makes it unberable. On top of that management is both slow and unable to come up with fixes to the accessibility requirements and any fixes need a rolling window of at least 1 year to be addressed.
column69: There aren't really alternatives. Specially during winter when icing conditions makes even access to buildings hard / dangerous.
column79: Mostly frustration at the lack of planning and concerns for the general population (students / staff).
column112: weel chair accessibility overal, changes to all doors for impaired access, bathroom access and indications
